Description: Compile-time checked bindings with better performance
Do: x:Bind for type-safe bindings

Description: Only instantiate UI elements when needed
Do: x:Load=False for hidden panels and dialogs

Description: Load list items in phases for smooth scrolling
Do: x:Phase on secondary content in DataTemplates
Don't: Loading all template content in phase 0
Code Bad: All content in single phase for complex templates

Description: Set default binding mode for a scope
Do: x:DefaultBindMode=OneWay on containers with many bindings

Description: WinUI 3 NavigationView with Left Top and LeftCompact display modes plus footer items
Do: NavigationView with PaneDisplayMode for main app shell
Code Bad: Custom SplitView with manual hamburger button

Description: Non-intrusive informational messages
Do: InfoBar for success warning and error messages

Description: Contextual tips attached to UI elements
Do: TeachingTip for feature discovery
Code Bad: Custom Popup positioned near target element

Description: Standard modal dialog pattern
Do: ContentDialog for confirmations and input
Code Bad: Grid overlay with manual focus trapping

Description: Show navigation path in hierarchical apps
Do: BreadcrumbBar for folder or category navigation

Description: Override control sub-properties via resources
Do: Lightweight styling resource keys to tweak controls
Don't: Full ControlTemplate override for small changes
Code Bad: Full ControlTemplate copy to change background color

Description: Consistent Fluent Design colors and brushes
Do: WinUI theme resource keys for colors

Description: Respect user and system theme preference
Do: ThemeResource for theme-adaptive values
Don't: Hardcoded colors that break in dark mode

Description: Acrylic Mica Reveal and rounded corners
Do: Built-in Fluent materials and effects

Description: Microsoft.UI.Xaml.Controls.Frame for WinUI 3 page navigation
Do: Frame.Navigate with page types and parameters

Description: Type-safe data passing between pages
Do: Typed parameter in OnNavigatedTo
Don't: Dictionary or string parsing for parameters
Code Good: protected override void OnNavigatedTo(NavigationEventArgs e) { var item = (Item)e.Parameter; }

Description: WinUI 3 uses NavigationView.BackRequested instead of UWP SystemNavigationManager
Do: Register NavigationView.BackRequested handler and manage back stack

Description: Handle protocol activation so URIs route to the right page
Do: Register protocol then check ExtendedActivationKind.Protocol on activation
Don't: Single entry point ignoring activation context

Description: Notifies UI of collection changes
Do: ObservableCollection<T> for bound ItemsSources

Description: Enable property change notification for UI updates
Do: INotifyPropertyChanged on ViewModels
Code Good: public string Name { get => _name; set => SetProperty(ref _name, value); }
Code Bad: public string Name { get; set; } without notification

Description: Call methods directly in bindings
Do: x:Bind with method references for transforms
Code Bad: IValueConverter class for bool to visibility

Description: x:Bind defaults to OneTime not OneWay
Do: Mode=OneWay or Mode=TwoWay when updates needed
Don't: Forgetting Mode and getting stale UI

Description: Virtualizing layout with full control
Do: ItemsRepeater for custom list layouts
Don't: ListView for highly customized item layouts
Code Bad: ListView with heavily modified template and removed chrome

Description: Load data on demand as user scrolls
Do: ISupportIncrementalLoading for large data sets
Code Bad: await LoadAllItems() on page load for 10K items

Description: Simpler trees render faster
Do: Minimal nesting in DataTemplates
Don't: Deeply nested panels in item templates

Description: x:Bind generates code at compile time
Do: x:Bind for hot paths and list items
Don't: {Binding} in DataTemplates and frequently updated UI

Description: WinUI 3 uses Microsoft.UI.Dispatching.DispatcherQueue instead of UWP CoreDispatcher
Do: DispatcherQueue.TryEnqueue for UI thread access

Description: Keep UI responsive during file and network access
Do: async/await for IO so the UI thread keeps rendering

Description: Offload compute to thread pool
Do: Task.Run for heavy computation
Don't: Long-running CPU work on UI thread

Description: Windows App SDK provides the runtime
Do: WinAppSDK NuGet package and WindowsAppSDK bootstrapper

Description: Choose deployment model for your scenario
Do: Packaged (MSIX) for Store distribution
Code Bad: Assuming all APIs work in unpackaged mode

Description: Simplified packaging for single app
Do: Single-project MSIX packaging
Don't: Separate WAP project when not needed
Code Bad: Separate Windows Application Packaging project for simple apps

Description: Enable Narrator and screen reader support
Do: AutomationProperties.Name on all interactive controls

Description: Full keyboard accessibility
Do: Tab navigation and access keys for all controls

Description: Screen readers use headings for navigation
Do: AutomationProperties.HeadingLevel on section headers
Don't: All text at same heading level

Description: Respect system high contrast settings
Do: ThemeResource brushes that adapt to high contrast

Description: Validate accessibility compliance
Do: Accessibility Insights for Windows scanning
Code Good: Run Accessibility Insights FastPass on every page

Description: Source generators reduce boilerplate
Do: [ObservableProperty] and [RelayCommand] attributes

Description: Register services with Microsoft.Extensions.DI
Do: IServiceProvider for ViewModel and service resolution
Don't: new ViewModel() and new Service() everywhere

Description: Start with proven architectural templates
Do: Template Studio for WinUI 3 project scaffolding
Don't: Blank project with manual setup for complex apps
Code Good: WinUI 3 Template Studio with MVVM and navigation
Code Bad: Blank App template for production app

Description: Keep business logic in .NET Standard or shared libraries
Do: Business logic in separate class library
Don't: Business logic mixed with WinUI types
Code Good: Shared.Core project with no WinUI references

Description: Proper window lifecycle management
Do: AppWindow API for multi-window scenarios
Don't: Single Window assumption in complex apps
Code Bad: Relying solely on MainWindow for everything

Description: Test logic independent of UI framework
Do: xUnit or MSTest on ViewModel properties and commands
Code Good: [Fact] public async Task LoadItems_PopulatesCollection() { await vm.LoadCommand.ExecuteAsync(null); Assert.NotEmpty(vm.Items); }
Code Bad: Manual testing by running the app

Description: Automated UI testing for WinUI 3
Do: WinAppDriver or Appium for end-to-end tests

Description: Isolate tests from platform dependencies
Do: Interface wrappers around WinRT APIs
Don't: Direct WinRT API calls in testable code

Description: Built-in numeric entry with validation formatting and spin buttons
Do: NumberBox with Minimum Maximum and SpinButtonPlacementMode
Don't: TextBox with manual numeric parsing and validation
Code Good: <NumberBox Value="{x:Bind Quantity, Mode=TwoWay}" Minimum="0" Maximum="100" SpinButtonPlacementMode="Inline"/>
Code Bad: TextBox with regex validation for numbers

Description: Expandable content area with header for progressive disclosure
Do: Expander for settings groups and optional content
Code Bad: Button toggling StackPanel.Visibility for collapsible content

Guideline: Use ProgressRing and ProgressBar for loading
Description: Built-in loading indicators for determinate and indeterminate states
Do: ProgressRing for indeterminate and ProgressBar for determinate progress
Don't: Custom spinning animation or text-based loading indicators

Description: Adapt UI layout to window size using adaptive triggers
Do: AdaptiveTrigger with MinWindowWidth for responsive breakpoints
Don't: Fixed layouts that break at different window sizes
Code Bad: Fixed two-column layout at all window sizes

Description: WinUI 3 apps receive activation events for URI and notification launches
Do: Check LaunchActivatedEventArgs in OnLaunched for activation context
Don't: Ignoring activation arguments losing deep link context
Code Good: protected override void OnLaunched(LaunchActivatedEventArgs args) { if (args.Arguments.Contains("settings")) NavigateToSettings(); }
Code Bad: Empty OnLaunched ignoring all activation parameters

Description: Prevent multiple app windows competing for resources
Do: AppInstance.FindOrRegisterForKey for single-instance enforcement
Code Good: var instance = AppInstance.FindOrRegisterForKey("main"); if (!instance.IsCurrent) { await instance.RedirectActivationToAsync(args); }
Code Bad: No instance management allowing duplicate windows

Description: Persist UI state across app restarts for continuity (ApplicationData APIs require packaged apps; unpackaged apps must use file IO or registry)
Do: Save state to local settings on window close or navigation
Don't: Losing user context on every restart
Code Bad: No state persistence losing navigation position on restart

Guideline: Choose Mica vs Acrylic by surface lifetime
Description: Mica is for long-lived primary surfaces like main windows; Acrylic is for transient light-dismiss surfaces like flyouts and context menus
Do: Mica on root window backgrounds and Acrylic on flyouts and overlays
Don't: Acrylic on the main window or Mica on transient flyouts
Code Bad: Acrylic on every Window background causing battery and perf cost

Description: WinUI 3 1.3+ exposes Window.SystemBackdrop with MicaBackdrop and DesktopAcrylicBackdrop classes replacing manual MicaController plumbing
Do: Window.SystemBackdrop in XAML or code
Don't: Hand-rolled MicaController wiring when SystemBackdrop API is available

Guideline: Open secondary windows with new Window
Description: WinUI 3 supports multiple top-level windows; each Window owns an AppWindow accessible via Window.AppWindow for size and position control
Do: new Window().Activate() for secondary windows tracking them in App state
Don't: Faking multi-window via main-window content swaps or ContentDialog
Code Bad: MainWindow.Content = new SettingsView(); when a separate window is needed

Guideline: Extend client area into the title bar
Description: Use Window.ExtendsContentIntoTitleBar with SetTitleBar to host custom XAML in the chrome while preserving caption buttons
Do: ExtendsContentIntoTitleBar=true plus SetTitleBar(element) for custom drag region
Don't: Hardcoded chrome height or custom caption buttons that break with theme and size changes
Code Bad: Padding="0,32,0,0" to reserve space without SetTitleBar leaves window non-draggable

Description: Map Ctrl/Alt/Shift combinations to commands using KeyboardAccelerator on UIElement
Do: KeyboardAccelerator with Modifiers and Key on relevant controls

Description: Share styles and brushes via App.xaml MergedDictionaries instead of duplicating per page
Do: MergedDictionaries in App.xaml for shared styles brushes and colors
Don't: Duplicating SolidColorBrush definitions on every page
Code Bad: SolidColorBrush Color hardcoded inline on every page

Description: AsyncRelayCommand exposes IsRunning and supports cancellation for IO bound work
Do: [RelayCommand] on async Task method or AsyncRelayCommand for IO work
Don't: async void event handlers or fire-and-forget Task.Run from button click
Code Good: [RelayCommand] private async Task LoadAsync(CancellationToken ct) { Items = await _service.FetchAsync(ct); }
Code Bad: private async void Button_Click(object s, RoutedEventArgs e) { await LoadAsync(); }

Description: Microsoft.Extensions.Logging ILogger<T> with DI for structured leveled logs
Do: ILogger<T> injected via constructor for diagnostic logging
Don't: Debug.WriteLine or Console.WriteLine for app diagnostics
Code Good: public MainViewModel(ILogger<MainViewModel> logger) { _logger = logger; } _logger.LogInformation("Loaded {Count} items", count);
